sql_array: CREATE TABLE test_table (id INT, timestamp_col VARCHAR, interval_col INTERVAL, value_col NUMERIC);
INSERT INTO test_table (id, timestamp_col, interval_col, value_col) VALUES (1, '4/15/2023 10:56:00', INTERVAL '5 DAYS', 100.0);
INSERT INTO test_table (id, timestamp_col, interval_col, value_col) VALUES (2, '5/15/2023 10:56:00', INTERVAL '10 DAYS', NULL);
INSERT INTO test_table (id, timestamp_col, interval_col, value_col) VALUES (3, 'invalid date', INTERVAL '3 MINUTES', 300.0);
INSERT INTO test_table (id, timestamp_col, interval_col, value_col) VALUES (4, '', INTERVAL '1 DAY', 200.0);
SELECT id, try_strptime(timestamp_col, ['%d/%m/%Y %H:%M:%S', '%m/%d/%Y %H:%M:%S']) AS parsed_timestamp, value_col FROM test_table WHERE value_col IS NOT NULL ORDER BY parsed_timestamp;

SELECT id, try_strptime(timestamp_col, ['%d/%m/%Y %H:%M:%S', '%m/%d/%Y %H:%M:%S']) - interval_col AS adjusted_timestamp FROM test_table WHERE try_strptime(timestamp_col, ['%d/%m/%Y %H:%M:%S', '%m/%d/%Y %H:%M:%S']) IS NOT NULL;

SELECT id, COUNT(*) AS count FROM (SELECT id, (try_strptime(timestamp_col, ['%d/%m/%Y %H:%M:%S', '%m/%d/%Y %H:%M:%S']) - interval_col) AS adjusted_timestamp FROM test_table) AS subquery GROUP BY id HAVING count > 0;

SELECT a.id, (try_strptime(a.timestamp_col, ['%d/%m/%Y %H:%M:%S', '%m/%d/%Y %H:%M:%S']) - a.interval_col) AS new_time FROM test_table a JOIN test_table b ON a.value_col = b.value_col WHERE a.id <> b.id OR a.value_col IS NULL;
SELECT DISTINCT (try_strptime(timestamp_col, ['%d/%m/%Y %H:%M:%S', '%m/%d/%Y %H:%M:%S']) - INTERVAL 10 DAY) AS final_time FROM test_table WHERE value_col IS NOT NULL;

CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_table AS SELECT id, try_strptime(timestamp_col, ['%d/%m/%Y %H:%M:%S', '%m/%d/%Y %H:%M:%S']) AS parsed_time FROM test_table WHERE try_strptime(timestamp_col, ['%d/%m/%Y %H:%M:%S', '%m/%d/%Y %H:%M:%S']) IS NOT NULL;
